description_en: The rōmon (two-story gate) of Aizen-in temple. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (structure) by Itako City on 1976-12-10. Located at Mizuhara 1121.
The Kannon-dō (Kannon hall) of Aizen-in temple. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (structure) by Itako City on 1976-12-10. Located at Mizuhara 1121.
The Hokke Zanmai-dō, a hall for Lotus Sutra meditation. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (structure) by Itako City on 1976-12-10. Located at Tsukiji 601.
The Jizō-dō (Jizō hall) of Fumon-in temple. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (structure) by Itako City on 1993-04-23. Located at Suzaki 603.

name_en: Monju-in temple halls (Amida, Kannon, and Jizō halls)
description_en: The Amida, Kannon, and Jizō halls of Monju-in temple. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (structure) by Itako City on 1993-04-23. Located at Ōga 627.
The saiden (ritual hall) of Ōu Shrine. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (structure) by Itako City on 1994-04-27. Located at Ōu 814.
An ema, a painted votive tablet. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (painting) by Itako City on 1969-12-03. Located at Mizuhara 1121.
A painted image of Fudō Myōō (Acala). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (painting) by Itako City on 1972-06-10. Located at Mizuhara 1121.

name_en: Painting of the Nirvana of Shakyamuni
description_en: A nehan-zu, a painting depicting the death (parinirvāṇa) of Shakyamuni Buddha. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (painting) by Itako City on 1972-06-10. Located at Mizuhara 1121.

name_en: Painting of the Waterfall-Viewing Kannon with dragon and tiger
description_en: A painting of the Waterfall-Viewing Kannon together with a dragon-and-tiger composition. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (painting) by Itako City on 1972-06-10. Located at Itako 428.
A matching pair of hanging scrolls in the Tosa-school painting style. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (painting) by Itako City on 1986-05-15. Located at Tsukiji 601.
A wooden seated sculpture of Fudō Myōō (Acala). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Itako City on 1967-08-31. Located at Ueto 624.
A wooden standing sculpture of Shō Kanzeon Bosatsu (Āryāvalokiteśvara). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Itako City on 1967-08-31. Located at Ueto 166.
A seated statue of the priest Nichiren. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Itako City on 1986-05-15. Located at Tsukiji 601.

name_en: Framed plaque of the Lotus Samādhi hall
description_en: A framed tablet (gaku) of the Hokke Zanmai-dō. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Itako City on 1986-05-15. Located at Tsukiji 601.
Wooden standing sculptures of the Twelve Heavenly Generals (Jūni Shinshō). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Itako City. Located at Horinouchi 1230.

name_en: Standing Bishamonten (Vaiśravaṇa) with halo and rock pedestal
description_en: A standing sculpture of Bishamonten (Vaiśravaṇa), including its halo (kōhai) and rock pedestal (iwaza). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Itako City on 1994-04-27. Located at Itako 428.
A sculpture of Fudō Myōō (Acala) held at Chōraku-in temple. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Itako City on 1997-11-21. Located at Itako 304.
A Buddhist statue of Daiitoku Myōō (Yamāntaka). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Itako City on 1997-11-21. Located at Tsukiji 127-1.
A sculpture of Kongō Aizen Myōō (Rāgarāja). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (sculpture) by Itako City on 2003-05-26. Located at Mizuhara 1121.

name_en: Maki-e tray and bowls with the embracing-peony crest
description_en: A set of lacquered tray and bowls decorated in maki-e with the dakibotan (embracing peony) crest. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Itako City on 1967-08-31. Located at Horinouchi 1230.
A shakujō, a Buddhist priest's ringed staff. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Itako City on 1972-06-10. Located in Ōga.
A figure of Benzaiten (Sarasvatī) made of molded ash. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Itako City on 1972-06-10. Located at Mizuhara 1121.
A Goryeo (Korean) tea bowl decorated with a cloud-and-crane (unkaku) design. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Itako City on 1976-12-10. Located at Itako 428.
An inkstone box (suzuribako) decorated entirely in maki-e lacquer. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Itako City on 1986-05-15. Located at Tsukiji 601.
A vestment box (hōebako) decorated entirely in maki-e lacquer. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Itako City on 1986-05-15. Located at Tsukiji 601.
A kyōzutsu, a cylindrical container for burying sutras. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (craft) by Itako City on 1986-05-15. Located at Tsukiji 601.

name_en: Great Perfection of Wisdom Sutra of Hiyoshi Sannō Shrine
description_en: A set of the Daihannyakyō (Great Perfection of Wisdom Sutra) held at Hiyoshi Sannō Shrine. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Itako City on 1967-08-31. Located at Nagayama 1176.

name_en: Calligraphic text of the Prajñā (Hannya) Sutra
description_en: A calligraphic transcription of the Hannya (Prajñā) Sutra text. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Itako City on 1969-12-13. Located at Mizuhara 1121.

name_en: Letter in the hand of Toyotomi Hideyoshi (the Taikō)
description_en: A personal letter attributed to the Taikō (Toyotomi Hideyoshi). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Itako City on 1969-12-13. Located at Itako 428.

name_en: Calligraphy scroll by Zen master Hakuin
description_en: A hanging calligraphy scroll brushed by the Zen master Hakuin. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Itako City on 1972-06-10. Located at Itako 428.
An autograph writing in the hand of the priest Nichiren. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Itako City on 1986-05-15. Located at Tsukiji 601.
An autograph writing in the hand of the priest Nichimon. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Itako City on 1986-05-15. Located at Tsukiji 601.

name_en: Fragment of an autograph letter by Saint Nichiren
description_en: A fragment of an autograph letter (shōsoku) in the hand of the priest Nichiren. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Itako City on 1986-05-15. Located at Tsukiji 601.
A single letter written by Tokugawa Mitsukuni. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Itako City on 1986-05-15. Located at Tsukiji 601.

name_en: Lotus Sutra in gold on indigo paper, one set with opening and closing sutras
description_en: A one-set copy of the Lotus Sutra written in gold pigment on indigo paper, including its opening and closing sutras (kaiketsu). Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Itako City on 1986-05-15. Located at Tsukiji 601.
An autograph writing in the hand of the priest Nichirin. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Itako City on 1986-05-15. Located at Tsukiji 601.

name_en: Great banner of the Gion Festival
description_en: A large banner (ōnobori) used in the Gion festival. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (calligraphy) by Itako City on 1988-06-21. Located at Itako 350.
A kakitomechō, a record book documenting the Nobukata sumo tradition. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (old document) by Itako City on 1976-12-10. Located at Shingū 1885.
An omizuchō, a land survey register. Municipally designated as a tangible cultural property (old document) by Itako City on 1987-07-14. Located in Ōga.
